exemplar.title: To what extent will the use of Polylactic Acid bioplastics allow businesses to improve their environmental and economic sustainability in the UK?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent will the use of Polylactic Acid bioplastics allow businesses to improve their environmental and economic sustainability in the UK?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive definitions and contextual framing.
The research question is clearly stated, well-focused and drives both environmental and economic strands.
An appropriate range of relevant secondary sources is selected, showing evidence of informed selection.
weaknesses: Minor lapses in phrasing lead to occasional informal tone.
Methodology relies solely on secondary data; no primary data collection limits depth of insight.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets all descriptors for band 5â6 in Focus and Method. The interdisciplinary topic is framed and explained with precision, the research question is specific, well-integrated and maintains focus throughout, and the methodology outlines an appropriate, varied range of sources with clear rationale. Only very minor phrasing issues and lack of primary data prevent a higher distinction, so a top-band score of 6 is justified.

strengths: Source material is generally relevant and integrated through paraphrase, quotation and figures.
Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, demonstrating effective understanding of chemical and business concepts.
Terminology and key concepts are used accurately and in context for the most part.
weaknesses: Occasional missing or informal citations weaken academic rigor.
Depth of economic analysis is sometimes surface-level, lacking detailed quantitative modelling.
Terminology slips (âitâs strong chainsâ) and occasional generic phrasing reduce consistency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently applies mostly relevant source material and shows clear subject knowledge, fitting band 3â4. Terminology is accurate overall but not entirely consistent, and there are a handful of missing in-text references and limited quantitative depth. These aspects justify a solid but not top-band score of 4.

strengths: Research draws on a varied, current set of interdisciplinary sources directly tied to the research question.
Analysis interprets chemical equations and economic data with sophistication, supporting clear conclusions.
The argument is well-developed, balancing environmental and economic perspectives with critical evaluation.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is unevenâLCA discussion remains partly descriptive and could be expanded quantitatively.
A few key claims lack full referencing (e.g. atom economy calculation errors, mixed data scopes).
Counter-arguments and ethical trade-offs receive only brief treatment, limiting the highest level of critical evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall Critical Thinking performance sits at the top end of band 7â9 and approaches band 10â12. The research is consistently relevant and well-synthesized, analysis is sophisticated in key areas, and the discussion forms an excellent, reasoned argument. Minor inconsistencies in referencing and uneven depth prevent a perfect 12, so a high mark of 10 accurately reflects the quality shown.

strengths: The report structure is clearly appropriate, following EE requirements with logical sectioning that aids comprehension.
Layout conventions (APA citations, figure captions, TOC, page numbering) are largely applied correctly.
Headings and flow from environmental to economic strands are coherent and reader-friendly.
weaknesses: Several figures are pixelated or lack standard captions and consistent numbering.
Some TOC entries miss page numbers, and long URLs break across lines.
A few editing oversights (incomplete sentences, inconsistent bibliography ordering) detract from polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation largely meets the criteria for band 3â4: the structure is fully appropriate and most layout conventions are correctly applied. However, recurring formatting inconsistencies and editing gaps prevent a top-band 4, so a mark of 3 is most fitting.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is presented verbatim on the title page and again in the conclusion.  It is specific (PLA bioplastics, UK businesses, extent of improvement in two dimensions of sustainability) and clearly drives the whole discussion: section I tackles environmental aspects, section II tackles economic aspects.  Focus is maintained; digressions are limited.  Because some sub-questions (e.g. micro-plastic impact, price elasticity) are hinted at but not rigorously pursued, the focus is strong rather than exemplary, justifying a score of 5 rather than 6.

The discussion links evidence to the RQ and ends with a balanced judgement (greater environmental than economic benefit).  Some critical points are raised (infrastructure gaps, farmland pressure), yet evaluation of counter-arguments is brief and the final conclusion largely reiterates earlier points.  Quality fits âwell-developed argument with partially critical evaluationâ, so a 4 is appropriate.

The student demonstrates accurate chemical knowledge (polycondensation, esterification, atom economy), business concepts (circular economy, CSR, SWOT) and sustainability frameworks.  Explanations are coherent, but depth is sometimes surface-level; for instance, the LCA discussion remains qualitative after the resource stage, and economic analysis omits NPV or cost-benefit calculations.  Sources are used but not always critically interrogated.  This reflects the middle of band 3â4.

Terminology such as âpolymerisationâ, âhydrolysisâ, âcircular business modelâ, âprice volatilityâ, and âcarbon emissionsâ is used accurately and in context.  Occasional slips (âitâs strong chainsâ, âlovaltyâ) and some generic phrasing in the CSR section reduce consistency.  Overall usage fits âmostly accurateâ, warranting a 4.

The essay offers clear lines of analysis: chemical equations interpreted, atom-economy compared, price differentials quantified, SWOT elements discussed.  However, depth is uneven; the LCA section remains descriptive after identifying stages, and cost comparisons lack detailed financial modelling.  Conclusions are supported but not rigorously evidenced, aligning with the lower end of band 4â6 (score 4).

Source material (Statista graphs, World Bank blog, BusinessAnalitiq price data, academic LCA papers) is generally appropriate and integrated through paraphrase, quotation and figures.  Citations support most assertions, though a few statistics (e.g. 83 % food-related waste) lack clear in-text referencing.  Application is therefore âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ relevant, meriting band 3â4 and a score of 4.

A good range of secondary sources (industry price indices, academic articles, LCA studies, government tax documents) is used and chemical equations are integrated.  Selection appears mostly relevant and partially interdisciplinary.  However, there is no primary data collection, and some key quantitative claims (e.g. atom-economy figures) rely on single, dated sources.  Evidence of critical source selection is moderate rather than âclear and effectiveâ, aligning with band 3â4.

Research covers chemistry, environmental science and business sources that are directly tied to the RQ.  While wholly secondary, it is varied and current (2023â24).  Relevance is clear, yet the absence of primary data and a few unreferenced claims limit it to solid rather than excellent quality, placing it mid-band 4â6.

Most layout conventions (APA-style citations, figure captions, TOC with page numbers, page numbering) are correctly applied.  Minor issuesâsome pixelated images, inconsistent figure numbering, and occasional long URLs breaking linesâprevent full polish, so a 3 (upper-middle of band) is justified.

The student states a clear interdisciplinary topic (âenvironmental and economic sustainability of PLA bioplastics for catering packagingâ) in the title page and reiterates it in the introduction.  Key terms such as âenvironmental sustainabilityâ, âeconomic sustainabilityâ and âbioplasticsâ are defined, and the scope (UK catering sector, PLA vs PET) is explicitly delimited.  Explanations of why the issue matters (pollution, fossil-fuel dependence, cost volatility) are woven into the opening narrative, showing comprehensive communication.  Minor lapses in phrasing and a slightly lengthy contextual background prevent a perfect 6, but the descriptor for band 5â6 is substantially met.

The essay follows the EE required structure: title page with word count, abstract substituted by succinct intro, table of contents, well-labelled sections, conclusion and full bibliography.  Logical flow from environmental to economic strands is clear and aids reader comprehension.  Hence the structure is âclearly appropriateâ, earning top-band marks (4).

annotations.comment: The title clearly communicates an interdisciplinary focus on environmental and economic sustainability of PLA bioplastics, effectively framing the investigation.
The research question is specific and well integrated, guiding both environmental and economic strands of the essay.
Word count is provided at the start, meeting the formatting requirement and ensuring compliance with limits.
Page numbering in the footer is present, supporting navigation and meeting formatting standards.
The table of contents structure is clear and logically ordered, aiding reader comprehension.
Several entries (e.g., SWOT main header) lack page numbers, reducing usability of the contents page.
The introductory paragraph references Ritchie et al. without a year; add complete in-text citations to strengthen academic rigor.
Figure 1 appears pixelated and its caption formatting is inconsistent; consider replacing with higher-resolution image and standardizing style.
The final sentence of the introduction is truncated; complete the thought to ensure clarity of the environmental problem statement.
Methodology section outlines secondary sources well but lacks any primary data collection; consider including a survey or interviews.
The description of hydrolysis vs glycolysis lacks quantitative recycling efficiency data; include figures to support analysis.
The atom economy calculation for PLA appears mathematically incorrect; revisit molar masses and recalculate accurately.
The LCA resources section introduces the concept well; consider adding a summary table of impact categories and values.
PET vs PLA processing descriptions describe energy qualitatively; include comparative COâ emission figures for deeper analysis.
The overall summary segment is descriptive but could better link conclusions explicitly back to the research question.
The economic sustainability introduction is conceptually sound but would benefit from more precise definitions supported by theory citations.
Figures 3 and 4 lack explicit captions or labels; add descriptive captions to clarify their roles in the circular economy discussion.
Recycling rate calculation mixes UK PET data with a global PLA statement; ensure data scope consistency for valid comparison.
SWOT analysis strengths section integrates price volatility and policy incentives effectively to support economic sustainability arguments.
Definitions of sustainability are well integrated, but the citation lacks a page or date reference; include full details in-text.
The phrase âit's strong chainsâ contains a contraction and is informal; revise to âits strong polymer chainsâ for precision.
The 83% figure for food-related waste is presented without an inline reference; add citation to underpin this key statistic.
The URL in the figure caption breaks across lines; adjust line breaks or use a DOI for cleaner formatting.
The âOpportunitiesâ subsection ends mid-sentence, indicating an editing oversight; complete the thought and proofread thoroughly.
The SWOT conclusion effectively synthesizes strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats to support your final economic judgement.
The CSR analysis describes pyramid tiers well but stops short of linking back to your specific research question; connect CSR findings to RQ.
The ethical responsibility section is descriptive but lacks critical evaluation; consider discussing ethical dilemmas or trade-offs.
The final evaluation reflection shows engagement with interdisciplinary methods but would benefit from more detailed analysis of methodological limitations.
Several bibliography entries lack publisher information or DOIs; standardize citations and include missing details for completeness.
URLs in references are inconsistently formatted; adhere to a single citation style (e.g., APA) for improved presentation.
The use of a Wikipedia article in the bibliography requires justification; consider citing more authoritative sources or explaining its inclusion.
Bibliography entries on pages 23â25 are not alphabetically arranged; sort entries to enhance navigability and professionalism.
